title: Resource implementation guidance routes legacy polling migrations through the custom poller skill
description: A synthetic implementation-guidance scenario where a contributor needs to replace legacy `pluginsdk.StateChangeConf.WaitForStateContext()` polling with a custom poller. The correct guidance should preserve polling parity, require a `pollers.PollerType` migration path, and include both the primary implementation skill marker and the custom-poller companion skill marker.

scope.notes: Synthetic fixture models a narrow polling-migration request after the custom-poller skill was wired into the shipped Go implementation workflow.

mustCatch.description: The guidance should replace the legacy `StateChangeConf` flow with a `pollers.PollerType`-based custom poller rather than leaving the legacy polling structure in place.
The guidance should explicitly preserve the legacy polling interval, pending/target semantics, and terminal error behavior unless the user approves a behavior change.

mustNotFlag.description: The guidance must not treat the task as ordinary CRUD/schema work only and ignore the specialist polling-migration workflow.

mustHaveSections: minimal set of code changes needed

notes: Use this as the adjudicated implementation-guidance benchmark for routing legacy polling migrations through the shipped custom-poller companion skill.
